steps.title: Identify the barrier effect to test
steps.content: The hypothesis states freshwater acts as a barrier preventing sea stars from climbing. To support it, the experiment must show sea stars climbing freely without freshwater but being blocked when it's present.

steps.title: Match the barrier evidence
steps.content:
The matching choice demonstrates this directly: seawater-only sea stars climb to the top, while sea stars in the freshwater tank stop just below the layer, showing that the freshwater blocks them.

A, C, and D each describe differences between the groups, but they do not show fresh water acting as a barrier: A says no sea stars climbed to the tops of the tanks, C says both groups reached the tops, and D says sea stars in the freshwater-layer tank climbed into the fresh water.

B is correct.
